- `what` can no longer stay inflated just because the minimizer is using stale thresholds
I’m rebuilding the service so the new pruning rule is what the running app uses going forward, not just the one-off repair I ran directly.
We’re at the target state now: 190 active cases, every phase gap closed, and every stem at or above 10% with `what` down to 14.2%.

The minimizer patch is in, but I caught one more detail: in this exact state, removing extra `what` questions is part of the solution too, so I’m widening the plateau-removal rule to allow that safely instead of only trimming secondary stems.
I’m fixing that at the pruning layer first, because that should get us to a smaller, balanced pool without generating more review work.
whatquestionsharemax
I’m doing one last targeted augmentation against those stems, then I’ll prune back any excess `what` so the pool stays minimal instead of growing for no reason.
The new share-based gaps are showing exactly what we need: we’re close, but still short on `who / when / where / why`.
I’m relaxing the prune rule so it can keep removing safe excess `what` cases even when the penalty only drops after several removals.
The last blocker is mathematical: with a share cap, removing one `what` case often doesn’t lower the penalty immediately because both the numerator and denominator move together.
I’m loosening that to allow gradual improvement, which is exactly what we need for a large `what` overage.
The only thing left is excess `what`.
That should make the next augmentation batch produce actor-focused questions more often instead of defaulting back to descriptive `what` forms.
The first controlled batch helped `when/why`, but `who` is still the main gap and `where` is still short.
That should let us raise the missing `who/when/where/why` counts in smaller controlled steps.
I’m running a targeted positive refill next so we add only the missing stem types, then I’ll prune back the excess `what` inventory.
The stricter targets are active now, and the pool is correctly showing the real imbalance: it’s short on `who/when/where/why` and heavily over on `what`.
The idea is simple: once the pool is gap-free, we greedily stale the most removable cases—starting with excess `what`—but only if the projected pool remains fully compliant after each removal.
question_stem_what
whatquestiongap
Then I’ll add a minimal-pool pruning pass that removes excess `what` cases only when the projected pool still stays fully gap-free.
I’m checking the current stem-target plumbing first, then I’ll finish the rebalance logic and prune excess `what` cases without reopening any phase gaps.

I’m moving the stem target from “light diversity pressure” to a hard floor now: 10% of the certification slice for each `what/who/how/when/where/why`, plus a lower `what` dominance cap so we can actually call the pool balanced.
I’ve confirmed the current pool is still too `what`-heavy for your use case, and the existing rule is too soft.

I’m tightening the stem rule to a real 10% floor for `what/who/how/when/where/why`, then I’ll shrink the active pool down to the smallest gap-free set instead of letting review inventory keep growing.
But 61% of what is still not considered balanced to me.
Remove cases if necessary (preferably the 'what' cases since there is inbalance there').
Set a minimum of 10% per stem type for what, who, how, when, where and why.
